mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.16% as of July 2009), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, mechanically inflating present-value valuations and producing persistent positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the policy rate stays near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% July 2009, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains under 0.5%, or forward returns during near-zero rate spells are not significantly above unconditional mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the monthly rise of UNRATE below a defined threshold signals that labor market deterioration is bottoming, reducing tail-risk pricing in equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors reprice a slower path of layoffs.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month increase falls below 0.2pp for two consecutive monthly releases
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5% July 2009 revised, up from 9.4% June, deceleration in pace of increase
regime_hint: labor market bottoming after recession peak
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where UNRATE MoM increase decelerates below 0.2pp for two months, 20d forward SPX returns are negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month upticks in INDPRO and RSAFS jointly signal a broad-based transition from contraction to expansion across both production and consumption channels, a combined confirmation that individually noisy single-series signals cannot provide, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO and RSAFS both near cyclical troughs in mid-2009
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery from industrial and consumption trough
failure_condition: Joint positive INDPRO and RSAFS monthly moves fail to produce 20d forward SPX returns exceeding the unconditional base rate in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC (<0.02) over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) stabilizing near cyclical lows after a sharp multi-year decline function as a leading indicator of demand recovery specific to post-recession regimes, where a bottoming HOUST series presages a broader consumption and investment rebound that lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level stabilizes within 5% of its trailing 6-month low for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 585K units June 2009, near multi-decade lows
failure_condition: HOUST stabilization signals fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns in more than 50% of post-recession historical episodes, or the effect reverses sign outside of recession-recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A shrinking pace of monthly PAYEMS declines below a critical threshold triggers a short-horizon relief rally in equities as markets price a reduced probability of an uncontrolled labor market collapse, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one trading week of the release.
candidate_trigger: monthly PAYEMS decline narrows to less than 300,000 jobs lost after prior months of losses exceeding 400,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS declining from 131,488K vintage to 130,662K revised, pace of losses moderating by mid-2009
regime_hint: labor market deceleration during recession exit
failure_condition: 5d forward SPX returns following a PAYEMS decline-narrowing event are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or occur negative in more than 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero FEDFUNDS and subdued CPIAUCSL inflation signals that the central bank retains policy room to sustain accommodation without inflation risk, reinforcing investor confidence in prolonged easing and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month; the interaction is necessary because low rates alone do not guarantee sustained ease if inflation is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 0.16% and CPIAUCSL 214.726 (low, stable inflation) as of July 2009
failure_condition: Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL joint low-rate/low-inflation state versus 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or effect is statistically indistinguishable from the FEDFUNDS-only signal.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects incremental improvement in consumer spending momentum that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a modest but persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised to 333,670M in July 2009, showing sequential stabilization after 2008-09 collapse
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and 5d forward SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO troughs during an economic recovery specifically emerging from a recession, the subsequent stabilization signals the start of an industrial upcycle that historically precedes multi-month equity rallies, but this relationship is regime-dependent and does not hold during late-cycle industrial slowdowns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised to 85.7027 in July 2009, near cyclical trough after prolonged 2008-09 decline
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery from recession-induced industrial trough
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns following an INDPRO trough-stabilization signal are not positive in more than 55% of historical recession-recovery episodes, or the effect sign flips outside recession-recovery regimes without being flagged as regime-conditional.
